role: Coordination nervous system — multi-agent message bus
completed: Append-only JSONL bus with resolve, I/O, validation, archival
MCP server with 10+ tools (signin, signout, heartbeat, post, read, send, archive, validate, resolve, main_check)
Supporter model: ADHD_ENABLE_SUPPORTER replaces exclusive coordinator claim/release/elect
ADHD_BUS_PATH clarified as storage directory prefix (default ~/.brainxio/adhd)
Tests with mocked path resolution and supporter coverage
.mcp.json aligned across all 5 repos with absolute paths
MCP change notification protocol (preparing/ready) on bus
Merge-queue claim protocol on bus (claim/release with 5-min TTL)
Bus path resolution: detect parent repo when inside git submodule via --show-superproject-working-tree
pyproject.toml aligned with BrainXio standard (pydocstyle, ruff format, slow marker)
HITL MCP server extension: human_claim_decision, human_provide_rpe, human_approve_gonogo, human_split_duties, pending_decisions, decision_history (on feat/hitl-bus-fixes branch, PR #12)

in_progress.subject: Expose ADHD rules/skills via MCP for self-describing bus usage
in_progress.description: Partial implementation exists on main branch (uncommitted): src/adhd/rules.py with full protocol schema, mcp_server.py wired with adhd_get_rules tool, and integration test. Must be committed AFTER feat/hitl-bus-fixes is merged, since rules.py references HITL tools that don't exist on main yet.
acceptance: New MCP tool adhd_get_rules returns structured protocol rules
Rules include: heartbeat interval, supporter model semantics, mcp-change protocol, env var descriptions
Rules are versioned and match the current bus schema
Tool is discoverable by MCP clients at runtime

pending.subject: Implement ADHD_PERF_LEVEL env var
pending.description: Add ADHD_PERF_LEVEL (low|medium|high, default medium) to supporter heartbeat payload. This allows task routing based on supporter capability. Static-only for now; detection helper is a future enhancement.
references: .archive/attention-deficit-hyperactivity-driver/ (old bus schemas, coordinator model pre-supporter, and PLAN.md showing the MCP-only migration rationale)
acceptance: bus.py reads ADHD_PERF_LEVEL and includes it in heartbeat payload
mcp_server.py heartbeat loop respects the env var
README and architecture docs document the env var

pending.subject: Add lightweight perf_level detection helper
pending.description: Create scripts/detect-perf-level.py (or similar) outside core MCP that probes hardware and suggests low/medium/high. Must not be imported by adhd-mcp. ADHD itself stays model-agnostic.
references: .archive/attention-deficit-hyperactivity-driver/PLAN.md (shows the hardware-agnostic mandate for core MCP)
acceptance: Helper checks nvidia-smi / sysinfo / cpu cores and prints suggested level
Explicit override (ADHD_PERF_LEVEL set) always wins
Documented as optional, not required

pending.subject: Implement Human-In-The-Loop (HITL) MCP server extension
pending.description: Extend ADHD MCP server with human-in-the-loop capabilities that reuse the existing JSONL bus. Four new tools: human_claim_decision (claim a pending decision), human_provide_rpe (provide reward prediction error feedback), human_approve_gonogo (approve/reject a Go/NoGo action), human_split_duties (split/supplement supporter duties). All state lives on the bus; no new databases.
acceptance: New bus message types: hitl_claim, hitl_rpe, hitl_approve, hitl_split
MCP tools exposed: adhd_human_claim_decision, adhd_human_provide_rpe, adhd_human_approve_gonogo, adhd_human_split_duties
State tracked via append-only bus messages (no DB, no cloud)
Tests cover claim/unclaim, rpe recording, approve/reject, and duty split

pending.subject: Implement HITL notification system (desktop + Telegram)
pending.description: When a human-in-the-loop decision is claimed on the bus, the human must be notified immediately. Implement desktop notifications via notify-send (Linux) and optional Telegram Bot API fallback. Provide a polling script (scripts/hitl-notify.py) that checks the bus for pending HITL decisions and alerts the human. Must gracefully degrade if neither channel is available.
acceptance: notify-send works on Linux desktop (ADHD_NOTIFY_URGENCY env var)
Telegram fallback via TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID env vars
Graceful degradation: logs warning if no notification channel available
Polling script supports --daemon mode with configurable interval
Tests for notification helpers (mocked subprocess / urllib)

pending.subject: Merge feat/hitl-bus-fixes into main and reconcile with adhd-7
pending.description: CRITICAL COORDINATION TASK. The feat/hitl-bus-fixes branch contains the complete HITL implementation (adhd-8), bus path fix, merge-queue protocol, and pyproject.toml alignment. Main branch has uncommitted adhd-7 work (rules.py + get_rules tool). The merge must happen first because rules.py on main references HITL tools that don't exist there yet. An agent must: (1) merge feat/hitl-bus-fixes into main, (2) verify rules.py references are accurate post-merge, (3) commit the adhd-7 work.
acceptance: feat/hitl-bus-fixes merged into main with no conflicts
adhd_get_rules tool returns accurate schema including HITL tools
All tests pass post-merge (pytest -q)
lint and format checks pass (ruff check ., ruff format --check .)

pending.subject: Add supporter stale-entry reaper
pending.description: Any supporter should periodically prune heartbeat entries older than 15 minutes to keep the active-supporters list accurate.
acceptance: Reaper function removes entries older than threshold
Called during archive or as separate maintenance tool

pending.subject: Document supporter model and perf_level
pending.description: Update README.md and docs/architecture.md to describe the supporter model, merge-queue protocol, and perf_level semantics. Keep the 'ADHD Parallel' tone.
acceptance: README tool table updated with merge-queue and perf_level notes
Architecture docs include merge-queue protocol section
